ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_00002473 covers bento as a food product category; the specific combination of teriyaki chicken, salmon, and prawn tempura is not individually coded. Closest parent match used.
ontology.foodex2_description: Composite dish — mixed protein bento with teriyaki chicken, salmon, and prawn tempura
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP covers composite dishes; no exact code exists for this specific bento combination. Closest parent match used and documented.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this specific Japanese bento combination dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard bento set portion is approximately 400 g, comprising teriyaki chicken, salmon fillet, prawn tempura, steamed rice, and vegetable sides as typically served in a Singapore Japanese casual dining context.

nutrient_source.title: HPB Singapore Foodo — Signature Bento Set (Teriyaki Chicken, Salmon, Prawn Tempura)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database for this specific dish (cr_id G00010133) with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, and trans fat not reported in HPB record; estimated from USDA FoodData Central composite analogy (teriyaki chicken + salmon + prawn tempura components) and documented in notes.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values — Atkinson et al. 2008; University of Sydney GI Database — mixed protein-rice composite analogous estimate
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific bento combination. GI estimated at 55 (low) based on the dominant carbohydrate source being teriyaki sauce sugars and trace batter carbohydrates (total carbohydrate 1.62 g/100g). The dish is protein-dominant and very low in carbohydrate; the GI of 55 is a conservative analogous estimate anchored to mixed protein-rice dishes in the University of Sydney GI Database. Glycemic load per 400 g serving = (55 × 6.48 g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 3.6 GL units, which is very low.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 723 mg/100g — exceeds the 600 mg/100g upper threshold for hypertension management
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this dish in its standard preparation; requesting reduced teriyaki sauce and omitting pickled vegetable sides can substantially lower sodium intake, but the baseline sodium load remains clinically significant.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low carbohydrate (1.62 g/100g) and sugar (1.47 g/100g) with high protein (25.73 g/100g) and omega-3-rich salmon supporting insulin sensitivity
type2_diabetes.guidance: This dish is suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed without a large rice portion; the prawn tempura batter adds refined carbohydrate and should be requested grilled as an alternative to further reduce glycemic impact.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High sodium (723 mg/100g) and deep-fried tempura component offset the cardioprotective benefit of salmon omega-3 fatty acids
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Salmon provides cardioprotective omega-3 fatty acids (EPA and DHA), but the high sodium from teriyaki sauce and the saturated and trans fat contribution from deep-fried prawn tempura elevate cardiovascular risk; requesting grilled prawns and reduced sauce is advised for individuals with cardiovascular disease.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-frying generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs) that worsen joint inflammation; sodium 723 mg/100g exceeds the 400 mg/100g RA threshold
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should request grilled prawns instead of tempura to eliminate AGE formation, and request significantly reduced teriyaki sauce to bring sodium below the 400 mg/100g RA threshold; trans fat at 0.1 g/100g is pro-inflammatory at any amount.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Signature Bento Set (Teriyaki Chicken, Salmon, Prawn Tempura) is a Japanese-style combination bento popular in Singapore casual dining. It features three protein components: teriyaki-glazed chicken thigh (juicy, caramelised, savoury-sweet), pan-seared or grilled salmon fillet (tender, rich in omega-3 fatty acids), and crispy deep-fried prawn tempura (light batter, crunchy exterior). The set is typically served with steamed Japanese short-grain rice and vegetable sides. Nutritionally, the dish is protein-dense at 25.73 g/100g and relatively low in carbohydrate at 1.62 g/100g, making it suitable for low-carbohydrate dietary patterns. However, sodium is high at 723 mg/100g driven by teriyaki sauce, which is a clinical concern for hypertension and rheumatoid arthritis. The salmon component contributes EPA and DHA omega-3 fatty acids with cardioprotective and anti-inflammatory properties. The deep-fried tempura component introduces saturated fat, trans fat (0.1 g/100g), and advanced glycation end-products. Energy density is moderate at 163 kcal/100g. This dish is a common lunch and dinner option in Singapore Japanese casual restaurants and food courts, and is culturally associated with Japanese bento culture adapted for the Singaporean market.

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010133, confidence 0.95, review_status reviewed). Saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, and trans fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central composite analogy and documented in notes. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database framework, documented accordingly.
notes: Saturated fat (1.4 g/100g), monounsaturated fat (2.1 g/100g), polyunsaturated_fat_g (1.8 g/100g), and trans_fat_g (0.1 g/100g) are estimated from USDA FoodData Central composite analogy for teriyaki chicken, salmon, and prawn tempura components (USDA FDC IDs: chicken thigh teriyaki ~171506 analogy, salmon cooked ~175168, shrimp tempura ~175177 analogy); these values are not directly reported in the HPB source record. Dietary fibre is 0.0 g/100g as reported by HPB; this is a confirmed zero for the protein-dominant components of this dish (chicken, salmon, prawn) with negligible fibre contribution from tempura batter. The following fields are set to -1 (data not available in HPB source or USDA analogy for this composite dish): vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g — detailed micronutrient and moisture data for this specific composite bento dish are not available in the HPB Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026. GI set to 55 (low) as an analogous estimate; the dish is very low in carbohydrate (1.62 g/100g) and glycemic load per 400 g serving is only 3.6 GL units, which is clinically negligible. The input record listed the condition key as 'diabetes'; this has been updated to 'type2_diabetes' with human_condition_name 'Type 2 Diabetes' per Veritas naming rules. The input record's rheumatoid_arthritis caution referencing trans fat 0.1 g/100g has been preserved in additional_conditions. This dish is sold in Singapore (region_tag SG) and therefore the full Singaporean multilingual rule applies: English (food_name), Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi local names are all populated. Japanese native-script name is also present as the dish is Japanese cuisine. Malay local name: Set Bento Istimewa (Ayam Teriyaki, Salmon, Udang Tempura). Tamil local name: சிறப்பு பென்டோ செட் (தெரியாக்கி கோழி, சால்மன், இறால் டெம்புரா). Hindi local name: सिग्नेचर बेंटो सेट (तेरियाकी चिकन, सैल्मन, प्रॉन टेम्पुरा). All aliases_by_language strings verified present verbatim in flat aliases array.
